metadata.notes: Probes categorization, taxonomy, and compare/contrast reasoning. Includes sorting tasks, superordinate probes, and 'flashcard'-style similarity/difference probes (e.g. sunflower vs sun). Difficulty 1=trivial, 5=expert.

stimuli.prompt: Group these items into meaningful categories and name each group: salmon, eagle, oak, dolphin, sparrow, pine, trout, willow, hawk, perch.

expected.note: dolphin is a mammal — accept either fish (visual category) or marine mammal as long as reasoning is given

stimuli.prompt: Group these items and name the groups: clarinet, drum, microscope, telescope, oboe, gong, tympanum, magnifying glass, binoculars, kazoo.
Group these and name the groups: water, helium, oxygen, mercury, hydrogen, ice, neon, glass, oil, argon.
Sort into groups and name them: French, Spanish, Mandarin, Italian, Cantonese, Portuguese, Japanese, Romanian, Korean, Catalan.
Sort into meaningful groups: chess, poker, soccer, basketball, bridge, go, tennis, volleyball, hearts, shogi.

expected.note: tennis can join volleyball/basketball as 'ball sports'

stimuli.prompt: Group and name: hammer, scalpel, screwdriver, syringe, pliers, stethoscope, wrench, otoscope, drill, forceps.
Group these and name the groups: mitochondrion, transistor, ribosome, capacitor, lysosome, resistor, nucleus, diode, golgi apparatus, inductor.
Group and name: Mercury, Mars, Jupiter, Earth, Pluto, Venus, Saturn, Eris, Neptune, Ceres.
Group these biological items: butterfly, frog, dragonfly, snake, ant, lizard, beetle, salamander, turtle, mosquito.
What kind of thing is a hippopotamus? Answer at the most useful general level (one or two words).
What kind of thing is a sonata? Answer at the most useful general level.
What kind of thing is a quark?
What kind of thing is a metaphor?
What kind of thing is a glacier?
What kind of thing is altruism?
What kind of thing is a fjord?
What kind of thing is a verb?
What kind of thing is osmosis?
What kind of thing is jazz?
How are a sunflower and the sun similar? How are they different? Provide both lists.

different_features: sunflower is alive (organic plant); sun is inanimate (a star)
sunflower is small (about 30 cm); sun is enormous (~1.4 million km)
sunflower is on Earth; sun is 150 million km away in space
sunflower has finite lifespan; sun has billion-year lifespan
sunflower follows the sun (heliotropism); sun emits light, doesn't receive it
sunflower is cool to touch; sun is plasma at thousands of degrees

stimuli.prompt: How are a river and a highway similar? How are they different? Provide both lists.

similar_features: both carry things from one place to another
both can be congested or flow freely
both shape the landscape they pass through
different_features: river is natural; highway is constructed
river carries water; highway carries vehicles
river flow is gravity-driven (one direction); highway flow is bidirectional
river is environmentally beneficial habitat; highway tends to fragment habitat
river requires no maintenance; highway requires constant maintenance
river surface is fluid; highway surface is solid asphalt

stimuli.prompt: How are a beehive and a city similar? How are they different? Provide both lists.

similar_features: both have a population that grows and shrinks
different_features: bees are genetically related (one mother); city residents are unrelated
hive has one queen; cities have many leaders
hive activities are largely instinctive; city activities are planned/cultural
hive size is modest; cities can have millions
hive products are biological (honey, wax); cities produce diverse goods
hive collapses without queen; cities are robust to leadership change

stimuli.prompt: How are a clock and a heart similar? How are they different? Provide both lists.

similar_features: both have an internal driving mechanism
different_features: clock is mechanical/electronic; heart is biological tissue
clock runs on stored energy or batteries; heart runs on metabolic ATP
clock can be repaired by replacing parts; heart cannot easily be replaced
clock keeps fixed time; heart rate varies with effort and emotion
clock can be paused and resumed; heart cannot easily be paused safely
clock is consciously designed; heart evolved

stimuli.prompt: How are a library and a forest similar? How are they different? Provide both lists.

similar_features: both contain many distinct items in a structured arrangement
both can be 'explored' in many paths
different_features: library is curated; forest is self-organized
library items are static; forest is dynamic/growing
library has indexed catalog; forest has no formal index
library is climate-controlled; forest is exposed to weather
library content is symbolic; forest content is biological
library is small; forest can span thousands of hectares

stimuli.prompt: How are a guitar and a typewriter similar? How are they different? Provide both lists.

similar_features: both are operated with the fingers
both produce output via discrete actions
both have many parallel actuators (strings / keys)
both make characteristic sounds during use
different_features: guitar produces music; typewriter produces text
guitar output is auditory; typewriter output is visual/material
guitar uses continuous pitch via fret choice; typewriter uses discrete fixed characters
guitar is portable performance instrument; typewriter is desk tool
guitar requires both hands doing different jobs; typewriter uses both hands symmetrically
guitar output is ephemeral; typewriter output is persistent on paper

stimuli.prompt: How are sleep and death similar? How are they different? Provide both lists.

similar_features: both are often associated with peace or stillness
both look similar from the outside
both have been linked metaphorically across cultures
different_features: sleep is reversible; death is irreversible
sleeping people still breathe and have heartbeat; dead bodies do not
sleep restores; death is the cessation of biological function
brain is highly active during sleep (especially REM); brain is silent in death
sleep occurs nightly; death happens once

stimuli.prompt: How is a smartphone similar to and different from a Swiss army knife? Provide both lists.

similar_features: both combine many functions in one device
both reduce the number of separate items needed
different_features: smartphone is electronic; Swiss army knife is purely mechanical
smartphone needs charging; knife needs no power
smartphone tools are software; knife tools are physical blades
smartphone is fragile; knife is durable
smartphone enables communication; knife does not
smartphone tools update; knife tools are fixed

stimuli.prompt: How are a thunderstorm and an argument similar? How are they different?

similar_features: both build up tension before erupting
both can be loud and dramatic
both can clear the air afterward
both can be brief or prolonged
both can damage things in their path
different_features: thunderstorm is meteorological; argument is interpersonal
thunderstorm is impersonal; argument involves intent
thunderstorm is shaped by physics; argument is shaped by psychology
thunderstorm is universally understood; argument depends on language and context
thunderstorm is observable from outside; argument may be private

stimuli.prompt: How are an ant colony and the internet similar? How are they different?

similar_features: both route information/resources between many nodes
both are robust to losing individual nodes
both exhibit emergent collective behavior beyond any single member
different_features: ant colony is biological; internet is electronic
ants act on local chemical cues; internet uses globally addressed packets
ant colony has fixed roles by caste; internet nodes can play arbitrary roles
ant colony evolved over millions of years; internet was designed in decades
ant colony has limited scale; internet spans the planet

stimuli.prompt: How are a candle and a star similar? How are they different?

similar_features: both consume fuel to do so
both have a finite lifespan determined by fuel
both glow brighter at the source and dimmer with distance
different_features: candle burns wax through chemical combustion; star fuses hydrogen via nuclear fusion
candle is small (centimeters); star is enormous (millions of km)
candle lasts hours; star lasts billions of years
candle is on Earth, fragile to wind; star is in space, unaffected by wind
candle emits visible-spectrum light; star emits across full electromagnetic spectrum

stimuli.prompt: How are language and music similar? How are they different?

similar_features: both are structured patterns of sound over time
both are produced and perceived by humans across cultures
both are learned (not purely innate) and culturally varied
both can be written down in symbolic notation
different_features: language has explicit semantic content; music's content is largely affective/abstract
language has discrete words/morphemes; music has continuous pitch/dynamics
language is mostly used for communication; music is often used for expression/aesthetics
language is processed in left-hemisphere areas predominantly; music engages broader bilateral networks
language has truth-conditional meaning; music does not
